hero.title: Free Online
hero.description: Extract and view detailed metadata from your photos. See camera settings, GPS location, date taken, and more—all processed locally in your browser.

alerts.fileSizeLimit: File size must be less than 50MB

dropzone.orClickBrowse: or click to browse a file
dropzone.maxSizeHint: Max 50MB • Supports JPEG, PNG, TIFF, HEIC

emptyExif.title: No EXIF Data Found
emptyExif.description: This image doesn't contain EXIF metadata. This can happen with screenshots, edited images, or images from social media.

messages.gpsHint: GPS is typically only in smartphone/GPS camera photos

features.privateDesc: All processing in your browser. Images never leave your device.
features.instantDesc: Get detailed EXIF data instantly. No server uploads needed.
features.completeDesc: View camera settings, GPS, timestamps, and all metadata.

howTo.title: How to View EXIF Metadata
howTo.step1Desc: Drag any JPEG, TIFF, or HEIC image into the viewer. It loads instantly in your browser — nothing is uploaded to a server.
howTo.step2Desc: Switch between Camera, Image, GPS, and All Data tabs to explore shutter speed, aperture, ISO, location, and every other metadata field.
howTo.step3Desc: Download the full metadata as a JSON file. Handy for archiving, forensic checks, or feeding into a build pipeline.
howTo.tipText: If you see "No EXIF Data Found" the image is likely a PNG, a screenshot, or was stripped by a social platform. Try the original file straight from your camera roll.

exifInfo.title: What Information is in EXIF?
exifInfo.p1: Every time a camera or smartphone captures a photo, it writes a hidden block of data alongside the pixel information. This metadata — called EXIF — records exactly how the shot was taken, when it happened, and sometimes where you were standing.
exifInfo.p2: Professional photographers use EXIF to learn from their own work: which aperture produced the sharpest result, whether the flash fired, and what lens was on the camera. Developers use it to auto-rotate images, geotag map markers, or verify timestamps in content moderation pipelines.

cards.cameraLensDesc: Make, model, lens name, and firmware version of the device that took the photo.
cards.exposureSettingsDesc: Shutter speed, aperture (f-stop), ISO sensitivity, focal length, and metering mode.
cards.gpsDesc: Latitude, longitude, and altitude — if location services were enabled at capture time.
cards.dateTimeDesc: When the photo was originally taken, when it was last modified, and the timezone offset.

items.question: What is EXIF data?
items.answer: EXIF (Exchangeable Image File Format) is metadata embedded in images by cameras and smartphones. It includes camera settings, date/time, GPS coordinates, and more.

items.question: Which image formats contain EXIF data?
items.answer: EXIF data is primarily found in JPEG and TIFF images. Some RAW formats and HEIF/HEIC images also contain EXIF data. PNG and GIF typically don't support EXIF.

items.question: Why might my image not have EXIF data?
items.answer: Some images may not have EXIF data because the software stripped metadata during editing, the device doesn't record EXIF, or social media platforms removed it for privacy.

items.question: Is my image uploaded to a server?
items.answer: No, all processing happens directly in your browser. Your images never leave your device, ensuring complete privacy and security.

items.question: What camera information can I find?
items.answer: EXIF typically includes camera make/model, lens info, exposure settings (shutter speed, aperture, ISO), focal length, flash, date/time, and sometimes GPS coordinates.
